top3.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM constructors c
JOIN constructorStandings cs ON c.constructorId = cs.constructorId
JOIN races r ON cs.raceId = r.raceId
WHERE c.nationality = 'Japanese' 
  AND cs.points = 0
  AND r.year = 2023
GROUP BY c.constructorId
HAVING COUNT(*) = 2;

SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM constructors c
JOIN constructorStandings cs ON c.constructorId = cs.constructorId
JOIN races r ON cs.raceId = r.raceId
WHERE c.nationality = 'Japanese' 
  AND cs.points = 0
GROUP BY c.constructorId
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT cs.raceId) = 2;

SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM constructors c
JOIN constructorStandings cs ON c.constructorId = cs.constructorId
JOIN races r ON cs.raceId = r.raceId
WHERE c.nationality = 'Japanese' 
  AND cs.points = 0
  AND r.year = 2023
GROUP BY c.constructorId
HAVING COUNT(*) = 2;

SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM (
    SELECT c.constructorId
    FROM constructors c
    JOIN constructorStandings cs ON c.constructorId = cs.constructorId
    JOIN races r ON cs.raceId = r.raceId
    WHERE c.nationality = 'Japanese' 
      AND cs.points = 0
    GROUP BY c.constructorId, r.year
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT r.raceId) = 2
) AS subquery;

SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM (
    SELECT c.constructorId
    FROM constructors c
    JOIN constructorStandings cs ON c.constructorId = cs.constructorId
    JOIN races r ON cs.raceId = r.raceId
    WHERE c.nationality = 'Japanese' 
      AND cs.points = 0
    GROUP BY c.constructorId
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT cs.raceId) = 2
) AS japanese_constructors_with_zero_points_in_two_races;

SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM constructors c
JOIN constructorStandings cs ON c.constructorId = cs.constructorId
JOIN races r ON cs.raceId = r.raceId
WHERE c.nationality = 'Japanese' 
  AND cs.points = 0
  AND r.year = 2023
GROUP BY c.constructorId
HAVING COUNT(*) = 2;

SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM (
    SELECT c.constructorId
    FROM constructors c
    JOIN constructorStandings cs ON c.constructorId = cs.constructorId
    JOIN races r ON cs.raceId = r.raceId
    WHERE c.nationality = 'Japanese' 
      AND cs.points = 0
    GROUP BY c.constructorId, r.year
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT r.raceId) = 2
) AS subquery;

SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM (
    SELECT c.constructorId
    FROM constructors c
    JOIN constructorStandings cs ON c.constructorId = cs.constructorId
    JOIN races r ON cs.raceId = r.raceId
    WHERE c.nationality = 'Japanese' 
      AND cs.points = 0
    GROUP BY c.constructorId
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT cs.raceId) = 2
) AS japanese_constructors_with_zero_points_in_two_races;

final.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM constructors c
JOIN constructorStandings cs ON c.constructorId = cs.constructorId
JOIN races r ON cs.raceId = r.raceId
WHERE c.nationality = 'Japanese' 
  AND cs.points = 0
  AND r.year = 2023
GROUP BY c.constructorId
HAVING COUNT(*) = 2;
